 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>GLOSSARY OF TERMS </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The terms defined in this section are used throughout this annual report on Form 10-K: </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:12px;margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="23%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="3%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="74%"></TD></TR>


<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Basin</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A large natural depression on the earth&#146;s surface in which sediments generally brought by water accumulate.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Bbl</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">One stock tank barrel of 42 U.S. gallons liquid volume is used herein in reference to crude oil, condensate or natural gas liquids.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">BBtu</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">One billion British thermal units.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Bcf</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">One billion cubic feet of natural gas.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Boe</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Barrels of oil equivalent using the ratio of six thousand cubic feet of natural gas to one barrel of oil.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Boe/d</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Barrels of oil equivalent per day.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Btu</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">British thermal unit, which is the heat required to raise the temperature of a one-pound mass of water from 58.5 to 59.5 degrees Fahrenheit.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Enhanced&nbsp;oil&nbsp;recovery&nbsp;(EOR)&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The use of any improved recovery method, including injection of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> or polymer, to remove additional oil after secondary recovery.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Field</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">An area consisting of a single reservoir or multiple reservoirs all grouped on, or related to, the same individual geological structural feature or stratigraphic condition. The
field name refers to the surface area, although it may refer to both the surface and the underground productive formations.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Horizontal drilling</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A drilling technique used in certain formations where a well is drilled vertically to a certain depth and then drilled at a right angle within a specified interval.</FONT></TD></TR>

<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Infill wells</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Wells drilled into the same pool as known producing wells so that oil or natural gas does not have to travel as far through the formation.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Limestone/carbonate</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A sedimentary rock composed primarily of calcium carbonate.&nbsp;It is an important reservoir rock for hydrocarbon production in the earth&#146;s subsurface.&nbsp;It can be composed
of various calcium carbonate grains or chemically precipitated.&nbsp;It often contains variable amounts of silica, silt, and clay.&nbsp;It is highly soluble which often results in secondary porosity and karsting.&nbsp;This can vary greatly from
place to place.&nbsp;These factors all generally make this rock type a more heterogeneous deposit than sandstone.</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">4 </FONT></P>

<TR>

<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>

acres.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>

<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The New York Mercantile Exchange.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">PDP</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Proved developed producing.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Play</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A term describing an area of land following the identification by geologists and geophysicists of reservoirs with potential oil and natural gas reserves.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Proved developed reserves</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Reserves that can be expected to be recovered through existing wells with existing equipment and operating methods, or in which the cost of the required equipment is relatively
minor compared to the cost of a new well.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Proved reserves</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The quantities of oil and gas which, by analysis of geoscience and engineering data, can be estimated with reasonable certainty to be economically producible&#151;from a given date
forward, from known reservoirs, and under existing economic conditions, operating methods, and government regulations&#151;prior to the time at which contracts providing the right to operate expire, unless evidence indicates that renewal is
reasonably certain.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Proved&nbsp;undeveloped&nbsp;reserves&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Reserves that are expected to be recovered from new wells on undrilled acreage or from existing wells where a relatively major expenditure is required for
recompletion.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">PV-10 value</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">When used with respect to oil and natural gas reserves, PV-10 value means the estimated future gross revenue to be generated from the production of proved reserves, net of estimated
production and future development costs, excluding escalations of prices and costs based upon future conditions, before income taxes, and without giving effect to non-property-related expenses, discounted to a present value using an annual discount
rate of 10%.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Sandstone</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A clastic sedimentary rock composed mainly of cemented sand-sized minerals or rock grains.&nbsp;Most sandstone is composed of quartz or feldspar.&nbsp;It is an important reservoir
rock for hydrocarbon production in the earth&#146;s subsurface. It is usually a more resistant rock than limestone and generally has less lateral variability in porosity.</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE>

<TR>
<TD WIDTH="18%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="80%"></TD></TR>


<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">SEC</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The Securities and Exchange Commission.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Secondary&nbsp;recovery&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The recovery of oil and natural gas through the injection of liquids or gases into the reservoir, supplementing its natural energy. Secondary recovery methods are often applied when
production slows due to depletion of the natural pressure.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Seismic survey</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Also known as a seismograph survey, it is a survey of an area by means of an instrument which records the vibrations of the earth. By recording the time interval between the source
of the shock wave and the reflected or refracted shock waves from various formations, geophysicists are able to define the underground configurations.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Spacing</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The distance between wells producing from the same reservoir. Spacing is often expressed in terms of acres, e.g., 40-acre spacing, and is often established by regulatory
agencies.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Unit</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The joining of all or substantially all interests in a reservoir or field, rather than a single tract, to provide for development and operation without regard to separate property
interests. Also, the area covered by a unitization agreement.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Waterflood</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The injection of water into an oil reservoir to &#147;push&#148; additional oil out of the reservoir rock and into the wellbores of producing wells. Typically a secondary recovery
process.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Wellbore</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The hole drilled by the bit that is equipped for oil or natural gas production on a completed well. Also called well or borehole.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Working interest</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The right granted to the lessee of a property to explore for and to produce and own oil, natural gas, or other minerals. The working interest owners bear the exploration,
development, and operating costs on either a cash, penalty, or carried basis.</FONT></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD HEIGHT="8"></TD>
<TD HEIGHT="8" COLSPAN="2"></TD></TR>
<TR>
<TD VALIGN="top" NOWRAP> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Zone</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">A layer of rock which has distinct characteristics that differ from nearby layers of rock.</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">6 </FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><A NAME="tx474623_1"></A>PART I </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Unless the context requires otherwise, references in this annual report to the &#147;Company,&#148; &#147;Chaparral,&#148;
&#147;we,&#148; &#147;our,&#148; and &#147;us&#148; refer to Chaparral Energy, Inc. and its subsidiaries on a consolidated basis. We have provided definitions of terms commonly used in the oil and natural gas industry in the &#147;Glossary of
terms&#148; at the beginning of this annual report. </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B><A NAME="tx474623_2"></A>ITEMS&nbsp;1. AND 2. BUSINESS AND PROPERTIES
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Overview </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We are a growing independent oil and natural gas production and exploitation company. Since our inception in 1988, we have increased reserves and production primarily through property acquisitions and
development activities. Our core operations consist of drilling for and production of oil and natural gas from conventional and unconventional reservoirs as well as a focus on tertiary operations through enhanced oil recovery (&#147;EOR&#148;)
projects utilizing CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> and polymer in the Mid-Continent and
Permian Basin areas. We maintain a portfolio of proved and unproved reserves, development and exploratory drilling opportunities, and EOR projects. Starting in 2011, we began to redirect our capital expenditures from the drilling of vertical wells
to the drilling of horizontal wells in repeatable resource plays and increased our level of expenditures on EOR projects. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As
of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had estimated proved reserves of 146.1 MMBoe with a PV-10 value of approximately $2.1 billion. These estimated proved reserves included 29.5 MMBoe of EOR reserves. Our reserves were 65% proved developed and 65% crude
oil. For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, our net average daily production was 25.0 MBoe, our estimated reserve life was approximately 16 years, and our oil and natural gas revenues were $509.5 million . We set forth our definition of PV-10
value (a non-GAAP measure) and a reconciliation of the standardized measure of discounted future net cash flows to PV-10 value on page 26. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">From 2003 to 2012, our proved reserves and production grew at a compounded annual growth rate of 12% and 15%, respectively. During this period, we have grown primarily through a combination of
developmental drilling and a disciplined strategy of acquiring proved oil and natural gas reserves, followed by exploitation activities and the acquisition of additional interests in or near these acquired properties. We have typically pursued
properties in the second half of their life with stable production, shallow decline rates and with particular producing trends and characteristics indicative of production or reserve enhancement opportunities. Since 2011, we have reduced the amount
of costs incurred for proved property acquisitions and spent more on acquisition of leasehold acreage and exploration costs in resource plays with repeatable drilling opportunities. In 1993, we began acquiring properties with CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR potential, and we have initiated CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection in 9 of these units to date. In 2005 and 2006, we completed
two larger acquisitions of $152.9&nbsp;million and $480.5 million, respectively, of oil and natural gas properties which contained substantial CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR potential and complemented our existing property base. We currently expect our long-term growth to come from the
development of our CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR operations, with our near term
growth coming from drilling activities. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">7 </FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Business Strategy </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We are positioned to grow our reserves and production profitably through our oil focused drilling activities,
primarily in our repeatable resource plays, in the near term and through our CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR projects in the long term. From 2003 to 2012, we have grown proved reserves and production by a compounded annual growth rate of 12% and 15%, respectively, through a combination of drilling and
acquisition success. Our reserve replacement ratio, which reflects our reserve additions from acquisitions, extensions and discoveries, and improved recoveries in a given period stated as a percentage of our production in the same period, has
averaged 383%&nbsp;per year from 2003 through 2012. We replaced approximately 156% of our production in 2012. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">As part of our
strategy to grow reserves and production profitably, we seek to serve as operator of the wells in which we own a significant interest. As operator, we are better positioned to control the (1)&nbsp;timing and plans for future enhancement and
exploitation efforts; (2)&nbsp;costs of enhancing, drilling, completing and producing the wells; and (3)&nbsp;marketing negotiations for our oil and natural gas production to maximize both volumes and wellhead price. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we
operated properties comprising approximately 85% of our proved reserves. We also strive to minimize commodity price risk through our financial hedging program. The principal elements of our strategy are described further below. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Focus drilling program on repeatable resource plays</I>.&nbsp;During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, we spent approximately
$268.6 million on drilling. We consider our repeatable resource plays to include the Northern Oklahoma Mississippi Play, the Anadarko Granite Wash, the Anadarko Cleveland Sand, the Anadarko Woodford Shale, the Panhandle Marmaton, and the Bone
Spring/Avalon Shale. During 2012, we spent $217.7 million of our drilling capital in these plays. Our drilling expenditures represented approximately 52% of our total capital expenditures for oil and natural gas properties and approximately 94% of
our increase in reserves related to purchases of minerals in place, extensions and discoveries, and improved recoveries for 2012. In 2013, we currently plan to spend approximately 58% of our capital expenditures, or $234.0 million, on drilling,
including $183.0 million in our repeatable resource plays mentioned above. As more fully discussed in the section &#147;Risk factors,&#148; our actual drilling activities may change depending on the availability of financing and capital, regulatory
approvals, seasonal restrictions, oil and natural gas prices, costs, drilling results and other factors. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">8 </FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Expand EOR activities</I>. We define EOR activities as activities on
properties that have proved EOR reserves, ongoing EOR operations, or that have an approved authorization for expenditure for EOR operations. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we have 11 active EOR projects including nine units where we are actively
injecting CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> and one project at our North Burbank Unit where
polymer is utilized. We plan to continue the polymer program and introduce CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> into the North Burbank Unit in 2013. During 2012, we spent $194.0 million, which was an increase from $88.2 million in 2011 and included $52.2 million classified as exploration costs, on the development
of our EOR assets. We have budgeted $137.0 million for development of our EOR assets in 2013. In 2014, our EOR capital investments are expected to increase somewhat but remain less than incurred in 2012 and should range between $75.0 million to
$150.0 million in subsequent years. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> used in EOR is an efficient method of producing crude oil. CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR involves the injection of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">, which mixes with the remaining oil in place in the producing reservoir, followed by the injection of water in alternating
cycles to drive the oil to producing wells and control gas processing rates, a process known as water alternating gas (&#147;WAG&#148;). Since we commenced CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection in the Camrick Unit in 2001, we have gradually increased our emphasis on EOR operations. Beginning in 2010, we
have further heightened our focus on this aspect of our business. During the past decade, we have learned a significant amount about the production of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">, transportation of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">, and EOR operations. Our EOR operations accounted for approximately 9% of our 2012 production and approximately 20% of our
proved reserves at December&nbsp;31, 2012. We believe
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">-based EOR has many advantages, including: (1)&nbsp;it has
a lower risk since we are working in fields that have substantial production histories and other historic data (i.e., known oil); (2)&nbsp;it provides a reasonable rate of return; and (3)&nbsp;we have limited competition for this type of activity in
our primary EOR project areas. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our active EOR projects are located in the Burbank area of northeast
Oklahoma (&#147;Burbank&#148;), the Panhandle areas of Oklahoma and Texas (&#147;Panhandle&#148;), Central Oklahoma (&#147;Central Oklahoma&#148;), and the Permian Basin Area in West Texas and Southeast New Mexico (&#147;Permian Basin Area&#148;).
In addition to our operated projects, we hold ownership interests in outside-operated CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> projects in the Panhandle Area, and have a small ownership interest in one outside-operated active EOR property in the Permian Basin Area. We currently have a total of 74 properties that we are analyzing
in regard to their CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR potential. To support our operated
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> projects, we have CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> supply agreements for the Panhandle and Central Oklahoma properties. We
have also developed a CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> pipeline infrastructure system with
ownership interests in 405 miles (245 net) of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> pipelines,
of which more than 328 miles are currently active. All of the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injected in our operated EOR units is anthropogenic (man-made) CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> which is captured from three different sources. A fourth source of
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> will be added in 2013 as we begin taking CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> from a fertilizer plant in Coffeyville, Kansas for injection in our
North Burbank Unit. We believe we are the largest and one of only a few CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR operators that use exclusively anthropogenic CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> from industrial manufacturing. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">9 </FONT></P>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Acquire properties for future growth</I>.&nbsp;In 2012, we redirected our acquisition
expenditures from mature properties with enhancement opportunities to prospect acreage in areas we consider to be repeatable resource plays. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our total acquisitions during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 were $48.0 million, including $1.1 million of proved reserve acquisitions, which represented approximately 0.1% of our increase in
reserves related to purchases of minerals in place, extensions and discoveries, and improved recoveries for 2012. We have budgeted $25.0 million, or 6% of our total capital expenditures, for acquisitions in 2013. We will continue to consider
individual field acquisitions that would complement our oil resource strategy. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Apply technical expertise to enhance mature
properties</I>.&nbsp;We seek to maximize the production and economic value of our base of mature properties through enhancement techniques and the reduction of operating costs. We have built our business around a strong engineering team with
expertise in the areas where we operate. We believe retaining our own field staff and operating offices close to our properties allows us to maintain tight control over our operations. We have 25 field offices in Oklahoma, Texas, Kansas and
Louisiana. Our personnel possess a high degree of expertise in working with lower pressure or depleted reservoirs and, as a result, are able to identify enhancement opportunities with low capital requirements such as installing a plunger lift,
pumping unit or compressor, as well as returning inactive wells to production by repairing various mechanical problems. Minimal amounts of investment have significantly enhanced the value of many of our properties. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, our
proved reserves included 840 shut-in and behind-pipe enhancement projects requiring total estimated capital expenditures of $78.6 million over the life of the reserves. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">10 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Maintain an experienced management team and strong investor support</I>.&nbsp;Mark
Fischer, our Chief Executive Officer and founder, has operated in the oil and natural gas industry for more than 40 years after starting his career at Exxon Mobil Corporation as a petroleum engineer. Joe Evans, our Chief Financial Officer, has over
35 years of experience in the oil and natural gas industry. Earl Reynolds, who became our Chief Operating Officer in February 2011, has 30 years of oil and natural gas production experience. Individuals in our 23-person management team have an
average of 30 years of experience in the oil and natural gas industry. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">CCMP Capital is a leading global private equity firm
with more than 21years in the energy industry, investing approximately $1.4 billion in energy over its history. CCMP Managing Director Chris Behrens joined our board of directors in 2010. Mr.&nbsp;Behrens has worked in private equity for 18 years
and leads CCMP Capital&#146;s energy investment activities. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Hedge production to stabilize cash flow</I>. Our long-lived
reserves provide us with relatively predictable production. To protect cash flows that we use for on-going operations and for capital investments, we enter into commodity price swaps, costless collars, and basis protection swaps. Derivative
positions are adjusted in response to changes in prices and market conditions as part of an ongoing dynamic process. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Based on
our year-end proved reserves estimated using SEC pricing as of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had derivative contracts in place for approximately 21% and 63%, respectively, of our estimated oil and natural gas liquids and natural gas production through
2014. During 2012, we received $11.8 million and in 2011 we paid $57.6 million on the net settlement of our derivative oil contracts and received $25.5 million and $34.1 million, respectively, on the net settlement of our derivative natural gas
contracts through a period of increasing oil prices and decreasing natural gas prices. During 2010, we received net derivative settlements of $40.0 million which included proceeds from early derivative monetizations of $7.1 million. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">11 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><B>Properties </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">The following table presents our proved reserves, PV-10 value as of December&nbsp;31, 2012, average net daily production for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, and average net daily production for the
quarter ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 , by our areas of operation. Reserves were estimated using a twelve-month average price, calculated as the unweighted arithmetic average of the first-day-of-the-month price for each month within the twelve-month
period prior to the end of the reporting period, unless prices were defined by contractual arrangements. Prices used as of December 31, 2012 were $94.71 per Bbl of oil and $2.76 per Mcf of gas. </FONT></P>

<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our properties have relatively long reserve lives and highly predictable production profiles. In general, these properties have extensive
production histories and production enhancement opportunities. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we owned interests in 8,243 gross (2,843 net) producing wells and we operated wells representing approximately 85% of our proved reserves. The high
proportion of reserves in our operated properties allows us to exercise more control over expenses, capital allocations and the timing of development and exploitation activities in our fields. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">12 </FONT></P>

<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">1,503</FONT></TD>

<TR BGCOLOR="#cceeff">
<TD VALIGN="top"> <P STYLE="margin-left:1.00em; text-indent:-1.00em"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Drilling and enhancements (in thousands)</FONT></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">$</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">193,959</FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">$</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">108</FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">$</FONT></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">194,067</FONT></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">&nbsp;&nbsp;</FONT></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We have been actively implementing and managing CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR in the Panhandle and Central Oklahoma Areas since 2001. We now have
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> supply agreements in place in the Burbank, Panhandle, and
Central Oklahoma Areas, and we have built and/or expanded CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
pipelines to reach our various field locations in the Panhandle and Central Oklahoma Areas. The CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> pipeline reaching the Burbank Area is scheduled for completion near the end of the first quarter of 2013. Arrangements to secure additional sources of CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> for potential future projects are currently in process. The U.S.
Department of Energy&#150;Office of Fossil Energy provided a report in February 2006 estimating that significant oil reserves could be economically recovered in Oklahoma and Texas through CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR processes. With our infrastructure, we believe that we will be well positioned to participate in the exploitation of
those reserves. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> miscible flooding is implemented and managed as a closed system consisting of the reservoir and surface piping.&nbsp;The
physical material balance of the system means that the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> is
produced and recycled many times once it has been injected.&nbsp;Combined with the incoming purchased CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> supply, the recycled CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> supply allows us to systematically develop additional flood patterns in contiguous acreage to the active patterns.&nbsp;If larger volumes of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> become available, projects can be developed on accelerated timelines, while a more limited supply dictates a prolonged
expansion.&nbsp;Due to the size of our EOR projects, the fixed rate of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> availability which is secured for the long-term period, and the limitation of third party services, we expect the development of our EOR projects will extend beyond five years in many cases. Our
significant active EOR projects are discussed below. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; margin-left:2%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Burbank Area </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>North Burbank Unit</I>.&nbsp;As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, the North Burbank Unit, which is our largest property,
accounted for 28.7 MMBoe, or 20% of our proved reserves, including 14.1 MMBoe of proved undeveloped polymer EOR reserves. The producing zones are the Red Fork and Bartlesville formations and occur at a depth of approximately 3,000 feet. We own a 99%
working interest in and are also the operator of this Unit. Our net average daily production from this Unit decreased by 7% from 1,455 Boe/d in 2011 to approximately 1,347 Boe/d in 2012, of which 46% or 49 Boe/d was due to a scheduled shut-in while
we raised pressure in the Phase I Area, the northwestern sector of the field. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had 296 (294 net) producing wells, 243 active injection wells, and 471 shut-in and temporarily abandoned wells in this Unit. Upside
potential exists in restoring a majority of the temporarily abandoned wells to production, expanding the polymer injection EOR program, and initiating CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR operations. Due to the size of the North Burbank Unit, there is insufficient fresh water and third party services
available to complete the development of the North Burbank Unit within five years, and as a result the development of the North Burbank Unit will be an ongoing project. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Phillips Petroleum Company instituted a polymer flood in their &#147;Block A&#148; polymer project that originally covered 1,440 acres. Production increased from 500 Bbls of oil per day to 1,200 Bbls of
oil per day in this original project area. The Phillips project was shut down in 1986 due to low oil prices. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">13 </FONT></P>



<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">In December 2007, we expanded a polymer flood into the Phase I area of the North Burbank
Unit, which consists of 485 acres adjacent to Block A on a 19-well pattern. During 2010, we completed a review of this project, and the results of this review confirm that injection of polymer is successful in recovering commercial quantities of
tertiary oil from the North Burbank Unit. Production has increased in this area from approximately 90 (78 net) Bbls of oil per day in 2008 to approximately 152 (132 net) Bbls of oil per day during 2012. Our review also indicated that additional oil
recovery may be obtained with additional volumes of polymer injected into the Phase I area. We implemented this additional injection into the Phase I area in 2011 and continued injection during 2012. </FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We have scheduled expansion of the polymer project from the Phase I area into Phase II during 2014 with development
of remaining phases to follow in future years as a sufficient volume of fresh water becomes available. We are also developing a Phase I CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> flood as we believe the field to have additional upside with the injection of CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">. On March&nbsp;24, 2011, we signed a 20-year contract with renewal
options to purchase up to 100% of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> emissions from an
existing nitrogen fertilizer plant in Coffeyville, Kansas that produces approximately 42 MMcf/d of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">. In early 2013, we expect completion of gathering and compression facilities at Coffeyville and a 68-mile pipeline to transport the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> to the Burbank field. Beginning no later than July 2013, and assuming the fertilizer plant produces and delivers a
specified quality of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">, we will be obligated to purchase an
average of approximately 24 MMcf/d the first year of the contract and 35 MMcf/d for the remaining contract years or pay for any deficiencies at the price in effect when the minimum delivery was to have occurred.&nbsp;After the first ten contract
years, we may permanently reduce up to 100% of our purchase rate under this contract with six months&#146; notice. We expect to purchase an average of approximately 24 MMcf/d of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> under this contract starting in the second quarter of 2013 for injection into our North Burbank Unit. As a result of our
success with polymer and additional technical analysis of the same, we are evaluating augmentation of our developing
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">-EOR project to include polymer during periods of water
injection within WAG cycles. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our total investment in the North Burbank Unit during 2012, including
expenditures associated with construction of the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">
compression facilities and pipeline, drilling two service wells, and reactivating 33 wells, was $141.0 million. Approximately $87.3 million of our capital expenditures budget for 2013 has been allocated for EOR operations in the North Burbank Unit.
</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; margin-left:2%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Panhandle Area </I></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Camrick Area Units</I>.&nbsp;As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, the Camrick Area Units accounted for 6.0 MMBoe, or 4% of our proved reserves, substantially all of which are considered EOR reserves.
Approximately 2.8 MMBoe of the proved reserves in this area are undeveloped. The Camrick Area Units consist of three units, which we operate: the Camrick Unit, where we began CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection in 2001; the North Perryton Unit, where we began CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection in 2006; and the NW Camrick Unit, which will be initiated as recycled CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> volumes become available from the other two Units. Currently, CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection operations are continuing in the Phase I and II areas of the
Camrick Unit and within the North Perryton Unit. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had 62 (37 net) producing wells, 47 active water injection wells, and 41 temporarily abandoned wells in this area. During the third quarter of 2010, we acquired an
additional 6% working interest in these three Units, thereby increasing our average working interest to 60%. Our net average daily production from this area increased 4% to 1,006 Boe/d in 2012 compared to 966 Boe/d in 2011, which was a 7% increase
compared to 902 Boe/d in 2010. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We have a long-term contract to purchase up to approximately
20&nbsp;MMcf/d&nbsp;of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> produced at an existing nitrogen
fertilizer plant in Borger, Texas. The fertilizer plant reserves the right to install additional equipment and use some of the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> to make certain fertilizer products, which could reduce our CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> purchases. We own 100% of and operate the 74-mile Borger CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> Pipeline, which transports CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> from the fertilizer plant to the Camrick Area Units. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we were injecting a combined
18&nbsp;MMcf/d&nbsp;of purchased and recycled CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> in this
area. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">Our total investment in the Camrick Area Units during 2012, including the purchase of CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> for injection, the drilling and completion of three wells, and general
well work, was $12.0 million. We have allocated approximately $8.8 million of our capital expenditures budget for 2013 to this area for continued purchase of CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">, drilling and completions, compression expansion, electrical upgrades, and remedial well work. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">14 </FONT></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Farnsworth Unit</I>.&nbsp;The Farnsworth Unit, which lies to the
southeast of and is analogous to the Camrick Area Units, accounted for 6.4 MMBoe, or 4% of our proved reserves, at December&nbsp;31, 2012. All of the reserves in this Unit, which includes 3.5 MMBoe of proved undeveloped reserves, are considered EOR
reserves as of December&nbsp;31, 2012. We acquired a 99% working interest in the Farnsworth Unit in November 2009 and we began CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection in December 2010. CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection has improved production in the Unit from approximately 394 (314 net) Bbls of oil per day in December 2011 to
approximately 1,093 (870 net) Bbls of oil per day in December 2012. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had 23 producing wells, 18 active water and CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB
STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection wells, and 44 shut-in and temporarily abandoned wells in the Unit. Our net average daily production from this
Unit increased 184% to 475 Boe/d in 2012 compared to 167 Boe/d in 2011, which was a 56% increase compared to 107 Boe/d in 2010. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We have a long-term contract to purchase up to approximately 15 MMcf/d of
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> produced at the Arkalon ethanol plant near Liberal,
Kansas. We have installed compression and purification facilities that are capturing approximately 14 MMcf/d of
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> from this plant as of December&nbsp;31, 2012. We own 100%
of and operate the 95-mile TexOk CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> Pipeline that includes a
CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> pipeline located between Liberal, Kansas and Spearman,
Texas. During 2010, we built a 14-mile pipeline extension through the Farnsworth Unit, connecting the TexOk CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> Pipeline and the Borger CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> Pipeline to allow flexibility in delivering CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> to the Farnsworth Unit and future projects. We completed the final tie-in of this extension in the first quarter of 2011. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we were injecting approximately
12&nbsp;MMcf/d&nbsp;of purchased and recycled CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> in the
Farnsworth Unit. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">During 2012, we invested $21.6 million in the Farnsworth Unit to drill two wells,
return four production and injection wells to service, complete associated field facilities work, and completed modifications at Arkalon. We have allocated approximately $23.6 million of our capital expenditures budget for 2013 to this Unit for the
continued development of the CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> project and related field
facilities. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Booker Area Units</I>.&nbsp;As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, the Booker Area Units accounted
for 0.9 MMBoe of our proved reserves, all of which are developed. In September 2009, we began CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection into our three Booker Area Units, which we operate with an average working interest of 99%. As of December&nbsp;31, 2012, we had 12 producing wells, six active water and CO</FONT><FONT
STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> injection wells, and three shut-in and temporarily abandoned wells in
the Unit. Our net average daily production from this area increased 473% to 402 Boe/d in 2012 compared to 70 Boe/d in 2011, which was a 55% increase compared to 45 Boe/d in 2010. Our total investment in the Booker Area Units during 2012 was $9.9
million, primarily spent on CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> purchases. We have allocated
approximately $9.6 million of our capital expenditures budget for 2013 to this area. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:18px;margin-bottom:0px; margin-left:2%"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"><I>Permian Basin Area </I></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6px;margin-bottom:0px; text-indent:4%;padding-bottom:0px; "><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">We have identified and own several potential projects in the West Texas and Southeast New Mexico area. Currently, we
do not have active operated CO</FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="1"><SUB STYLE="vertical-align:baseline; position:relative; top:.4ex">2</SUB></FONT><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2"> EOR projects, and have a
small ownership interest in one outside-operated active EOR property, the Adair San Andres Unit in this area. </FONT></P>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px"><FONT SIZE="1">&nbsp;</FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0px;margin-bottom:0px" ALIGN="center"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman" SIZE="2">15 </FONT></P>
